[package]
name = "fmrihrf"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2021"
authors = ["Bradley Buchsbaum <brad.buchsbaum@gmail.com>"]
description = "Rust implementation of fMRI Hemodynamic Response Function (HRF) and Regressor Tools"
license = "MIT OR Apache-2.0"
repository = "https://github.com/bbuchsbaum/fmrihrf-rs"
keywords = ["fmri", "hrf", "neuroimaging", "neuroscience", "signal-processing"]
categories = ["science", "mathematics"]

[dependencies]
thiserror = "1.0"
ndarray = { version = "0.15", features = ["blas", "rayon"] }
ndarray-linalg = { version = "0.16", features = ["openblas-static"] }
nalgebra = "0.32"
num-traits = "0.2"
rayon = { version = "1.7", optional = true }
serde = { version = "1.0", features = ["derive"], optional = true }
serde_json = "1.0"
lazy_static = "1.4"
sprs = "0.11"
approx = "0.5"
statrs = "0.16"
rustfft = "6.1"

# Plotting dependencies
plotters = { version = "0.3", default-features = false, features = ["bitmap_backend", "svg_backend", "ab_glyph", "bitmap_encoder", "line_series"], optional = true }
plotly = { version = "0.8", optional = true }

[dev-dependencies]
criterion = { version = "0.5", features = ["html_reports"] }
proptest = "1.2"
approx = "0.5"
pretty_assertions = "1.4"
rstest = "0.18"
tempfile = "3.8"

[features]
default = []
serde = ["dep:serde", "ndarray/serde"]
parallel = ["ndarray/rayon", "dep:rayon"]
plotting = ["dep:plotters", "dep:plotly"]


[profile.release]
lto = true
codegen-units = 1
opt-level = 3

[profile.bench]
debug = true

[[bench]]
name = "hrf_benchmark"
harness = false

[package]
name = "splines"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2021"
rust-version = "1.70"
authors = ["Generated from R splines package"]
description = "Fast, safe Rust implementation of R splines package functionality"
license = "MIT OR Apache-2.0"
repository = "https://github.com/user/splines-rust"
documentation = "https://docs.rs/splines"
categories = ["science", "mathematics", "algorithms"]
keywords = ["statistics", "splines", "interpolation", "bsplines", "numerical"]

[dependencies]
# Core data structures and linear algebra
ndarray = { version = "0.15", features = ["serde"] }
nalgebra = "0.32"
num-traits = "0.2"
num-complex = "0.4"

# Error handling
thiserror = "1.0"
anyhow = "1.0"

# Serialization
serde = { version = "1.0", features = ["derive"] }
serde_json = "1.0"

# Performance
rayon = { version = "1.8", optional = true }

# Logging
log = "0.4"

[dev-dependencies]
criterion = { version = "0.5", features = ["html_reports"] }
proptest = "1.4"
tempfile = "3.8"
approx = "0.5"
env_logger = "0.10"

[features]
default = ["std"]
std = []
parallel = ["rayon"]

[[bench]]
name = "performance"
harness = false

[profile.release]
lto = true
codegen-units = 1
overflow-checks = false

content.text:
Based on my analysis, I recommend adding the splines package as a path dependency since both packages are in your local development environment. This approach provides:

1. **Direct integration** - No need to publish the splines crate
2. **Shared development** - Changes to splines immediately available in fmrihrf-rs
3. **Compatible dependencies** - Both use ndarray 0.15, nalgebra 0.32, and similar versions

    22→/// Create the SPM canonical HRF.
    23→///
    24→/// This implements the canonical hemodynamic response function as defined in SPM 
    25→/// (Statistical Parametric Mapping), which is a difference of two gamma functions.
    26→/// The canonical HRF peaks around 5-6 seconds and has an undershoot around 15 seconds.
    27→///
    28→/// # Arguments
    29→/// * `a1` - Shape parameter for positive gamma (default: 6.0)
    30→/// * `a2` - Shape parameter for negative gamma (default: 16.0)
    31→/// * `b1` - Scale parameter for positive gamma (default: 1.0)
    32→/// * `b2` - Scale parameter for negative gamma (default: 1.0)
    33→/// * `c` - Ratio of negative to positive gamma (default: 1/6)
    34→/// * `normalize` - Whether to normalize to unit peak (default: true)
    35→///
    36→/// # Examples
    37→///
    38→/// ```
    39→/// use fmrihrf::hrf::spm_canonical;
    40→/// use ndarray::Array1;
    41→///
    42→/// // Create default SPM canonical HRF
    43→/// let hrf = spm_canonical(None, None, None, None, None, Some(true));
    44→/// 
    45→/// // Evaluate at time points
    46→/// let times = Array1::linspace(0.0, 30.0, 150);
    47→/// let response = hrf.evaluate(&times).unwrap();
    48→/// 
    49→/// // Response peaks around 5-6 seconds
    50→/// let peak_idx = response.iter()
    51→///     .enumerate()
    52→///     .max_by(|(_, a), (_, b)| a.partial_cmp(b).unwrap())
    53→///     .map(|(idx, _)| idx)
    54→///     .unwrap();
    55→/// let peak_time = times[peak_idx];
    56→/// assert!(peak_time > 4.0 && peak_time < 7.0);
    57→/// ```
    58→///
    59→/// # Custom Parameters Example
    60→///
    61→/// ```
    62→/// use fmrihrf::hrf::spm_canonical;
    63→///
    64→/// // Create HRF with slower response (larger a1)
    65→/// let slow_hrf = spm_canonical(
    66→///     Some(8.0),  // Slower rise
    67→///     Some(16.0), // Default undershoot
    68→///     None, None, None, 
    69→///     Some(true)
    70→/// );
    71→/// ```

## ✅ Integration Complete!

The splines library has been successfully integrated into your fmrihrf-rs project. Here's what was accomplished:

### 🔄 Replaced Implementations

1. **B-spline HRF basis** (`src/hrf/functions.rs:427-532`) - Replaced custom Cox-de Boor recursion with R-compatible splines library
2. **Cubic spline interpolation** (`src/hrf/empirical.rs:168-193`) - Replaced manual tridiagonal solver with splines library  
3. **Updated dependencies** (`Cargo.toml:26`) - Added path dependency to your splines package

### 🎯 Key Improvements

- **Better R compatibility** - Your splines library provides exact R behavior vs custom implementations
- **Robust error handling** - Graceful handling of out-of-range values and dimension mismatches
- **Maintained API compatibility** - All existing tests pass with no breaking changes
- **Enhanced functionality** - Leverages your splines library's validated algorithms

### 📊 Test Results

- **117/117 tests passing** ✅
- **B-spline functionality verified** ✅ 
- **Empirical HRF interpolation working** ✅
- **All integration tests successful** ✅

The integration preserves all existing functionality while providing better R compatibility through your dedicated splines library. Your fMRI HRF modeling will now benefit from the more robust and R-compatible spline implementations.
